subject: A stone construct shaped like an angular tripod or trestle, hewn from massive carved blocks engraved with runes, standing in a barren rocky landscape
description: A weathered stone construct stands centered in a barren outdoor setting, built from heavy rectangular slabs arranged into a wide-stanced tripod or sawhorse silhouette. The legs splay outward and meet under a long horizontal capstone, the whole structure pitted and chipped from age. Rune-carvings are visible along the surfaces of the stones — faintly luminous markings that hint at dormant magic. The ground is dusty rust-orange rock with scattered debris, the sky behind a flat dim gray-brown wash. The construct is silent, motionless, monumental — more like a relic or megalith than an active automaton.
foreground: rune-carved stone construct on rocky ground
background: flat barren wasteland under overcast sky
emotion: still, dormant, monumental — no living expression; the artifact reads as patient and inert
